llm_result.summary: The presented case shows a pattern of repeated lateness driven by underestimation of task durations, disorganized pre-departure preparation, lack of planned buffers, variability in routine task times, and consequent negative self-evaluation. Modifiable predictors should target (a) cognitive timing accuracy and external time cues (psycho); (b) preparatory routines and item readiness (social/environmental cues); (c) environmental/calendar adjustments and buffers (social); (d) self-regulatory skills and self-compassion (psycho); and (e) basic physiological supports that enhance alertness and consistency (bio). The proposed levers span Bio/Psycho/Social domains and their interactions, with concrete metrics and feasible timelines to observe improvements in lateness frequency (C01), time-estimation accuracy (C02), pre-departure preparedness (C03), buffer use (C04), task duration variability (C05), and self-criticism (C06). Each lever is designed to be changeable, implementable in routine life, and measurable with simple metrics.

solutions.predictor_variable: Time Estimation Training with External Time Cues
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured practice to estimate routine task durations (e.g., shower, key-finding, walking to car) with immediate feedback, paired with automatic on-screen/time-based alerts (alarms, countdowns) that compare estimated vs actual durations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces systematic underestimation of task durations, improving scheduling realism and reducing delays that lead to lateness.
solutions.measurement: Mean absolute error between estimated vs actual task durations; proportion of events started on time; weekly lateness rate.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns. Monitor for frustration with estimates; adjust pace if anxiety increases.
solutions.mapping_query: How does structured time-estimation training with external cues affect lateness frequency and duration estimation accuracy?

solutions.predictor_variable: Pre-Departure Organizational Cue System (Keys/Phone/Clothes Ready by Door)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a door-ready cue system: designated hook for keys/phone, standardized outfit/coat by door, and a 2-minute pre-departure micro-routine assisted by a physical prompt.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Ensures essential items are routinely prepared, reducing time wasted searching and delaying departures; creates a reliable pre-departure state.
solutions.measurement: Rate of items found/ready before departure; time from decision to leave; lateness proportion.
solutions.safety_notes: Simple physical setup; no controversial components.
solutions.mapping_query: Will door-ready cue systems improve pre-departure preparedness and reduce lateness?

solutions.solution_candidate: Block calendar buffers (e.g., 10-20 minutes) before each commitment; treat buffers as non-negotiable with automatic reminders.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Buffers absorb unpredictable delays, reducing late starts and perceived time pressure.
solutions.measurement: Buffer adherence rate; average delay avoided per event; weekly lateness rate.
solutions.safety_notes: Adjust buffer length as needed to avoid over-planning; monitor stress from tighter schedules.
solutions.mapping_query: Does scheduling built-in buffers before commitments reduce lateness frequency and improve on-time starts?

solutions.solution_candidate: Create simple, public-facing templates or checklists for common routines (e.g., shower routine, car-walk sequence) with time estimates and step order.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing variability by standardizing sequences lowers duration variability and underestimation errors.
solutions.measurement: Task duration SD per task; mean task duration; lateness rate.
solutions.safety_notes: Templates should be flexible enough to accommodate occasional deviations.
solutions.mapping_query: Will standardized routine templates reduce duration variability and underestimate errors?

solutions.solution_candidate: Brief self-compassion exercises and cognitive reframing to reduce negative self-evaluation after lateness (“mess” feelings) with daily reflection prompts.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decreases punitive self-talk that may undermine motivation and adherence to punctual routines; supports consistent behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Frequency/intensity of self-critical thoughts; correlation with lateness rate.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for resistance to self-compassion messaging; adapt language to individual preferences.
solutions.mapping_query: Can self-compassion and cognitive reframing reduce negative self-evaluation and improve on-time behavior?

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily lateness and duration-estimation logs with weekly feedback summaries; include trend visualization and goal tracking.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Providing real-time feedback enhances awareness, supports corrective actions, and reinforces desired routines.
solutions.measurement: Proportion of events on time; estimation accuracy error over time; adherence to logging.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure data privacy; avoid excessive monitoring that increases stress.
solutions.mapping_query: Does daily self-monitoring with feedback improve lateness and duration estimation accuracy?

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep Regularity and Morning Light Exposure
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent bed/wake times and morning light exposure to improve alertness and cognitive control in mornings.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep/circadian alignment enhances executive function and time perception, reducing lateness and underestimation.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/consistency; chronotype-adjusted wake times; lateness rate; duration estimation accuracy.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep disorders; refer if persistent insomnia or daytime fatigue.
solutions.mapping_query: Will sleep regularity and morning light exposure improve punctuality and time-estimation accuracy?

solutions.predictor_variable: Pre-Departure Light Physical Activation (Brief Morning Exercise)
solutions.solution_candidate: Short, 5-10 minute brisk activity before leaving (walk/jump rope) to boost arousal and cognitive readiness.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases waking energy and focus, potentially reducing timing errors and delays.
solutions.measurement: Subjective alertness; lateness rate; duration-estimation accuracy.
solutions.safety_notes: Safe for most adults; advise medical clarification if cardiovascular concerns.
solutions.mapping_query: Does brief pre-departure physical activity increase punctuality and duration estimation accuracy?

solutions.solution_candidate: Pair with a trusted contact to check in on punctuality, share calendars, and provide encouragement for on-time departures.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social accountability enhances commitment to punctual routines and buffer use.
solutions.measurement: Buddy-confirmed on-time starts; perceived accountability score; lateness rate.
solutions.safety_notes: Choose a supportive partner; avoid dependency creation.
solutions.mapping_query: Will a punctuality buddy improve on-time starts and buffer adherence?

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital Tools for Reminders and External Offloading
solutions.solution_candidate: Phone-based reminders, checklists, and geofenced prompts with simple offloading of memory tasks (keys, wallet, etc.).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: External cognitive aids reduce reliance on internal memory and support timely departures.
solutions.measurement: Prompt completion rate; rate of leaving after prompts; lateness proportion; estimation accuracy.
solutions.safety_notes: Respect privacy; configure non-intrusive prompts; allow opt-out at any time.
solutions.mapping_query: Do digital reminders and checklists improve pre-departure organization, buffer use, and on-time departures?
